record.text:
Daniel Benyamin Released New Album “Life After Music”

Daniel Benyamin’s Life After Music is more than an album—it’s a philosophical and artistic critique of modern music culture in the age of streaming and AI. Created in isolation at “Dolphin Palace,” a remote studio near Mount Olympus, the double…

external.description: Daniel Benyamin’s Life After Music is more than an album—it’s a philosophical and artistic critique of modern music culture in the age of streaming and AI. Created in isolation at “Dolphin Palace,” a remote studio near Mount Olympus, the double album explores themes of loneliness and space, life and silence, each thematic making the title of each side of a double vinyl. Benyamin argues that music today has become oversaturated and emotionally diluted by endless playlists, algorithmic recommendations, and AI-generated content. He questions whether people have truly “evolved” in how they listen, or whether they’ve simply forgotten how to listen deeply.
external.title: Daniel Benyamin Released New Album “Life After Music”

external.description: G.K. Butterfield Jr., a former Democratic representative from North Carolina, has raised concerns about a recent Supreme Court decision in Louisiana v. Callais, describing it as a 'five-alarm fire' for voting rights. The ruling permits states to create partisan gerrymandered maps even if these maps have a racially discriminatory effect, effectively removing the final protections under the Voting Rights Act for minority voters. While media coverage has largely focused on the impact of this decision on the upcoming midterm elections, Butterfield warns that the implications could be broader and more severe. Specifically, the ruling may allow states to eliminate local electoral districts, forcing minority candidates to compete statewide instead of within smaller districts, which historically allowed for fairer representation. Butterfield recounted personal experiences, noting that his father’s local office career ended after North Carolina replaced ward-based local elections with at-large systems in the 1950s, illustrating how election rules can profoundly influence outcomes. Butterfield expressed his desire to fight for voting rights as a response to these historical injustices and cautioned that the recent Supreme Court decision could silence minority voices and weaken democratic representation in local and state governments.

Cisco's AI orders forecast just hit $9 billion-and the stock surged

external.description: Shares of the networking giant in business for over 40 years surged more than 13% Thursday to an intraday record of $119.36, capping one of the most improbable turnaround stories in modern market history. The catalyst: a Q3 earnings report that showed CEO Chuck Robbins’ multi-year bet on AI infrastructure is paying off. Record revenue of $15.8 billion, up 12% year-over-year, topped the high end of guidance. Management raised the FY26 AI revenue target to $4 billion (from $3 billion) and lifted AI orders guidance to $9 billion (from $5 billion). Q4 revenue guidance of up to $16.9 billion came in above consensus, and non-GAAP operating income hit a record. The company also announced on Wednesday a Q4 workforce reduction of fewer than 4,000 jobs—less than 5% of total headcount. Cisco frames the cuts as an AI-driven strategic shift, which reallocates investment toward AI infrastructure, silicon, optics, and security, rather than AI directly replacing workers. Morningstar, which rates Cisco a wide moat, just raised fair value to $90 from $75. Founded in 1984, Cisco was once the backbone of the internet—and briefly the most valuable company in the world during the dot-com era, before the bubble burst. Read more about...

Russian Battalion Commander Eliminated by Drone Strike in Donetsk Region
->UNITED24 Media | More on "Russian battalion commander killed Ukraine" at BigEarthData.ai | #Drone #RussiaUkraineWar

external.description: A Ukrainian drone strike killed the commander of Russia’s “Yugra” battalion, Lt. Col. Oleg Rudomyotov, in the Donetsk region, Militarnyi reported on May 15. Rudomyotov’s death was confirmed by the Russian regional television channel Yugra, which had previously published interviews with him. Antarctica's sudden sea ice loss is one of the most extreme and confusing events in the modern climate record. Scientists now know why it's happening.
->Live Science | More on "Antarctica sea ice rapid decline" at BigEarthData.ai | #Climate #SeaIce #Science

external.description: Antarctica's sea ice started shrinking dramatically in 2015 after resisting global warming for decades, and researchers now know why. A study published May 8 in the journal Science Advances reveals that Antarctic sea ice succumbed to strong winds that disturbed the Southern Ocean's layers, replacing cold and relatively fresh surface water with warmer, saltier water that caused some initial melting. As sea ice declined over the years and reflected less sunlight back to space, the ocean absorbed more heat, thus accelerating the loss way beyond what scientists were expecting. The research identified three phases of Antarctic sea ice decline between 2013 and 2023. Previous data shows that sea ice extent hit a record low in February 2023 and that Antarctica was missing a chunk of ice bigger than Western Europe at winter's peak in July of the same year. The continent has not recovered since, with sea ice extent reaching another near record low in 2024 and remaining below the 1981-to-2010 average in 2025 and early 2026. "The system is behaving in a different manner," study first author Aditya Narayanan, a physical oceanographer at the University of New South Wales in Australia and the University of Southampton in the U.K.,...
external.title: Antarctica's sudden sea ice loss is one of the most extreme and confusing events in the modern climate record. Scientists now know why it's happening.

🎙️ Charaktere & Cast (Japanisch)

»Celene Yang« von M・A・O
Köchin im chinesischen Restaurant „Sea Dragon“ in Kobe. Ihr Vater stammt aus Hongkong, ihre Mutter aus Frankreich, geboren und aufgewachsen ist sie jedoch in Kobe. Mit ihrem Motto „Kochen muss richtig kräftig sein“ verkörpert sie eine lebensfrohe Kansai-Persönlichkeit. Sie kämpft dafür, eine neue Form chinesischer Küche zu verbreiten, die französische Elemente einbindet: „Nouvelle Chinoise“.

»Kei Sawada« von Takahiro Sakurai
Ein Koch aus dem chinesischen Restaurant „Kunlun“ in Roppongi. Für ihn ist Kochen Feuer. Im Gegensatz zu Jan ist er für sein gepflegtes Aussehen bekannt und besonders bei Frauen beliebt. Er ist auf Gerichte mit Feuer und Hitze spezialisiert, begeistert das Publikum mit übertriebenen Performances und besitzt zugleich das Können eines echten Handwerkers.

»Tomohide Jun« von Taishi Murata
Der Erbe des chinesischen Restaurants „Toyoro“ in Yokohama. Für ihn ist Kochen Tradition. Er entstammt einer alten Restaurantlinie mit über 400 Jahren Geschichte, einschließlich Geschäften auf dem chinesischen Festland. Obwohl er erst 16 Jahre alt ist, besitzt er bereits außergewöhnliche Fähigkeiten und tritt mit großem Stolz für den Namen seines traditionsreichen Hauses an.

»Yuji Kawahara« von Yūsuke Kobayashi
Der Sohn des Leiters der Kawahara Cooking School. Für ihn ist Kochen Wissenschaft. Er verfolgt die Idee einer „Küche des 21. Jahrhunderts“ und sieht sich selbst zugleich als Koch und Wissenschaftler. Statt auf Gefühl setzt er auf Analyse, Daten und modernste Küchengeräte, um das perfekte Gericht zu erschaffen. Damit steht er in starkem Kontrast zu Jan.

»Nichido Otani« von Masuo Amada
Ein Restaurantkritiker, der als „Mann mit der Zunge Gottes“ bekannt ist. Er besitzt einen miserablen Charakter, aber einen herausragenden Geschmackssinn und beurteilt Köche mit messerscharfer Wahrnehmung. Weil Jan ihn einst bloßgestellt hat, hegt er einen tiefen Groll gegen ihn und stellt ihm selbst als Juror immer wieder absurde, schwierige Aufgaben. Dadurch wird er zu Jans größtem Erzfeind.


📜 Beschreibung 》
Der junge Spitzenkoch Jan Akiyama arbeitet in einem renommierten Restaurant, doch für ihn ist die Küche kein Ort der Gastfreundschaft, sondern ein Schlachtfeld. Immer wieder fordert er Kiriko Gobanchou heraus, die Enkelin des Besitzers und selbst eine außergewöhnliche Köchin. Während Kiriko daran glaubt, dass jedes Gericht für den Menschen am Tisch entstehen sollte, sieht Jan im Kochen vor allem den Wettkampf. Zwischen beiden entbrennt ein hitziges Duell aus Talent, Stolz und völlig unterschiedlichen Vorstellungen davon, was gutes Essen wirklich bedeutet.

元気な挨拶、会話、ブレンダーのうなり、シュシュ…チリチリ…とスチームの音、豆を挽く音、そして流れるコーヒーの香りと音楽。すべてが心地よかった。

record.text:
Spent the morning sketching what "level 1 of an onboarding agent" should feel like.
Hardest part isn't the Copilot Studio side. It's deciding what a new hire should fail at and still feel good about.
Game design is sneaky like that.

Unpopular opinion: most "onboarding chatbots" are just FAQ pages with a personality disorder.
That's why we're rebuilding the whole thing from the ground up at #ScottishSummit2016 in October.
@sandrakiel.bsky.social

社内の予算が厳しいのと、GitHub Copilot の料金形態が変わるのとで、６月以降 GitHub Copilot がほとんど使えなくなる状態に｡｡｡＿|￣|○

なんなら IntelliJ も使えなくなりそうだ。。。
